You want to read this caption 

It was Saturday, July 15 a week or so after Fourth of July and I poured out the last little bit of alcohol. I had in my house and decided never again.

I had a large task in front of me. I had to build a six figure company in about 30 days to be able to afford to live out here and fight for my daughter’s custody.

I had everything stripped from me. I had shut down all my other business ventures to go all in on this business with my ex-wife.

And so when she put me on the street, I essentially had to whip up a six figure business in 30 days. There was no other option she had burned the boats. It wasn’t even my choice.

And faced with that, I had two choices I could lean into vices and slow slowly wait to die

Or I could become a man let go of boyish things remove the devices from my life and become everything she told me I never could be

outliers.source_caption:
Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t trust happiness.

You trust struggle.

A lot of people don’t realize they’ve spent so much time surviving that peace starts to feel suspicious.

The reading says that good feelings can actually make us anxious.

Not because something is wrong.

Because something is right.

And if you’ve spent years carrying stress, chaos, heartbreak, or uncertainty, joy can feel unfamiliar.

So when life finally starts getting better, you start looking for what’s wrong with it.

Maybe happiness isn’t the thing leaving.

Maybe you’re just not letting it stay.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

The real goal, the only metric that truly matters, is time. How many of your hours can you buy back each week? The whole point of building an online business is to get your life back, not to lose it. This happens when you build smart systems and automations that do the work for you. You build the engine once, so you can return to the field, to your family, to your life. Your business should exist to serve your life, not the other way around. If you want to build an online business that actually gets you offline, comment 'FieldWork' below and I'll send you my guide.

outliers.relevance_reason: Vague mention of 'real results for real coaches' without specifying income, time, or lifestyle benefits.
outliers.source_caption:
Two key takeaways from this…

But first an apology I am a very objective thinker and so presented with facts I drop opinions and I lean into what’s actually real

And I can’t deny he gets real results for real coaches and they’re very happy (for the most part)

My first observation would be that this program is clearly designed for coaches who are already various established. You need to have the teams the SOP‘s and the systems in place for ads to be able to run and you need to have clear amounts of profit to be able to throw into ads on top of the investment you’re making for this program to work well for you.

Second, there is a difference between the results that someone gets and the way that person presents themselves online. I’m a big fan of the results that Will is getting his clients. I’m not a huge fan of how he voices that online and his attitude towards other coaches in the industry.

I wanna see this industry grow over the next 10 years and I wanna see it grow into a place that we’re all proud to be a part of 

So I still stand by what I said when it comes to how he is dealing with other coaches in the industry in their containers

I do not agree with attempting to poach clients from other coaches. I do not agree with talking poorly about other coaches and that’s why I’m making this video.

I don’t like that I took a shot at him, especially without having all the information. And clearly I am at a 10th of Will’s size and so I’m just punching up Will not punching down still isn’t a great look and I don’t think it’s the most productive use of any of our times

So while I stand by what I said when it came to how Will is dealing with other coaches

I do respect the results he gets for his clients and how he chooses to talk about those is completely up to him

With all that being said, let’s keep the focus on what we are all trying to do here which is impact more lives through Fitness

but thank you to all the clients from Will’s program who commented on the video and help me get a better understanding of what’s going on inside the container
outliers.hook_framework: Fair Critique of a Contrasting Ideology
outliers.why_it_worked: This reel tapped into a relevant industry debate with a nuanced, high-road take. This approach builds authority and attracts an audience that values thoughtful critique over simple callouts.
outliers.headline: My Honest Take On Hustle Culture
outliers.recommended_angle: Offer a balanced critique of the 'growth at all costs' mentality. Acknowledge its effectiveness for a certain type of business, but firmly state why it's not the path FieldWork advocates for, focusing on the human cost and the 'Live Offline' alternative.
my_hooks: Two honest thoughts on the 'hustle' gurus.
First, a concession about the 24/7 grind.
Let's be fair: hustle culture gets results. But at what cost?
My two cents on the 'growth at all costs' model.
I need to get this off my chest about online business culture.
my_talking_points: I want to share two takeaways from the whole 'hustle culture' conversation online. But first, a point of agreement. I can't deny that for some people, that 24/7 grind produces massive financial results.

My first observation is that this model is really designed for a specific person in a specific season of life. It’s for founders who are willing and able to sacrifice nearly everything else, their time, their relationships, their health, for a singular focus on scaling.

Second, there is a big difference between a financial result and a human result. I can respect the revenue numbers they produce, but I don't personally align with the narrative that this is the only path to success.

I believe building a great business should also mean building a great life. I want to see a world where success is measured by the time we have for our families and our passions, not just our profit margins.

So I still believe that glamorizing the grind is a disservice to most founders, coaches, and experts out there. I don't agree with the idea that you have to burn yourself out to build something meaningful and profitable.
Look, my approach isn't for everyone, and that's okay. I'm not here to say the hustle guys are wrong, just that their way isn't the only way. It's not my way.
My focus will always be on using smart systems, AI, and automation to build an online business that buys back your time.
Because the entire point of building online is to live more offline. If that sounds like the right path for you, comment 'FieldWork' below and I will send you our guide on how to get started.
